qa_pair.question: Which Hermes tool inspects recent durable jobs without changing state?
Which Hermes tool should be called once at the start of a scheduled Work execution?
What must the executor do when hermes_claim_research_run returns a null job?
Which tool creates a new durable research objective?
Which tool persists the outcome of a claimed run as compact memory?
What are the two accepted final states for a committed run?
Do any of the four Hermes MCP tools interact with the open world directly?
What durable store holds the job queue and compact result memory?
Does this control plane promise a continuously running Hermes process?
qa_pair.answer: No. It implements hibernation across ephemeral Work runs.

qa_pair.question: What happens when a Work run claims a job and terminates before committing it?
qa_pair.answer: The two-hour claim lease eventually expires, and a later claim call automatically returns the job to the queue.
